items.author: Keerthika Uthayakumar
items.body_excerpt:

- the 5th-best defence in the NBA, 2nd-best clutch defence
- the 2nd-youngest rotation in the 2026 playoffs (weighed by minutes, top-8)
- a top-10 halfcourt offence (99.4)
- a team that went from 26th to 15th in offensive rating with Ingram

items.title: If you’re curious the best the Nuggets could do is a vet minimum with a player option for a second year.
items.author: Jake Shapiro
items.body_excerpt:
If you’re curious the best the Nuggets could do is a vet minimum with a player option for a second year.

They *could* with dumping one or two of their top six, offer a taxpayer mid-level which is like $6 million.

So no, LeBron ain’t coming to Denver.

items.title: Some quick fun facts about Kawhi Leonard this season:
items.author: Keerthika Uthayakumar
items.body_excerpt:
Some quick fun facts about Kawhi Leonard this season:
- 20+ points in 57 straight games, the 2nd-longest active streak in the NBA.
- 6th in points, 4th in steals, 4th in fast break points.
- Five 40-point games this season, 5 combined in his first 5 seasons with the Clippers.

items.title: The anticipatory reporting had Toronto as a place (maybe the only place) he'd extend, so I'd imagine that's forthcoming. Dealing picks always hurts, but Kawhi has played 2000 minutes two of the last three seasons and was a legit All-NBA First Team candidate last year. Raptors jus
items.author: Dan Devine
items.body_excerpt: The anticipatory reporting had Toronto as a place (maybe the only place) he'd extend, so I'd imagine that's forthcoming. Dealing picks always hurts, but Kawhi has played 2000 minutes two of the last three seasons and was a legit All-NBA First Team candidate last year. Raptors just got a lot better.

items.title: NBA deputy commissioner Mark Tatum said the NBA has landed on clear frontrunners in 12 cities for NBA Europe after it received final bids for the league's launch. That list of includes "existing basketball and football clubs." The NBA now will shift to finalizing agreements.

items.title: If the LA Clippers clear the decks of FA cap holds and non-guaranteed salaries, they can create over $31M in cap space this summer. They got off some money by trading Kawhi Leonard to the Toronto Raptors for Brandon Ingram and Gradey Dick.

items.body_excerpt: If the LA Clippers clear the decks of FA cap holds and non-guaranteed salaries, they can create over $31M in cap space this summer. They got off some money by trading Kawhi Leonard to the Toronto Raptors for Brandon Ingram and Gradey Dick.

items.title: The Clippers will save a little bit of money by trading Kawhi Leonard, but their cap situation remains mostly unchanged.
items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
The Clippers will save a little bit of money by trading Kawhi Leonard, but their cap situation remains mostly unchanged.

They could have $26 million in cap space. They currently have more spending power as an over-the-cap team.

They now have a surplus of 9 first-round picks.

items.title: Keep an eye on Kawhi Leonard's trade bonus for the Raptors. If he waives the bonus, Toronto should have room to fill out the roster with veteran minimums while staying under what will now be a first-apron hard cap.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
Keep an eye on Kawhi Leonard's trade bonus for the Raptors. If he waives the bonus, Toronto should have room to fill out the roster with veteran minimums while staying under what will now be a first-apron hard cap.

This likely closes the door on Sandro Mamukelashvili returning.

items.title: Fun little note: Brandon Ingram was traded a year and a half ago for basically Keaton Wagler.
items.author: Jake Shapiro
items.body_excerpt:
Fun little note: Brandon Ingram was traded a year and a half ago for basically Keaton Wagler.

That deal was between the Raptors and Pelicans, involving a Pacers pick and now the two will play for the Clippers together.

The NBA, lol!

items.title: Tarris Reed Jr. when asked about having a veteran of the 2026 NBA Finals on hand to help show him and other #Spurs rookies the ropes at Summer League.
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt:
Tarris Reed Jr. when asked about having a veteran of the 2026 NBA Finals on hand to help show him and other #Spurs rookies the ropes at Summer League.

"How old is he? Twenty? He's a young cat. It's crazy," Reed, 22,  said of Carter Bryant, 20.
